title: How People Influence Each Other
period: Everyday social influence, leadership, and persuasion
summary: How trust, respect, listening, and ego-management shape persuasion more effectively than criticism, force, or argument.

context.headline: Influence starts with human psychology, not pressure
context.description: People are not persuaded only by logic. They protect pride, seek recognition, and respond to those who make them feel respected and understood. This module turns social influence into a practical model: reduce defensiveness, build trust, guide thinking, and support change without humiliation.
context.when: Daily life, teams, leadership, and negotiation
